description: Bears workspace governance plugin for Spec Kit orchestration, role gates, blockers, deploy gates, contracts, validators, hooks, Git/CD policy, project registry gates, and workflow closeout. Codex daemon and Knowledge Orchestrator runtime implementation live in BearsCLOUD/codexdaemon.

defaultPrompt: Run the Bears workflow overlay for this feature with role gates, blockers, deploy gates, roadmap-control gating, plan/tasks analysis, and implementation control.

Run the plugin constitution gate after route gate and before research for plugin governance changes; validate assets/catalog/plugin-constitution.v1.json with `python3 scripts/plugin_constitution.py validate` and inspect change packets with `python3 scripts/plugin_constitution.py inspect-change --packet <path>`.

Run the deterministic research gate before Spec Kit specification, plan, tasks, analyze, and implementation for broad, new, risky, drift-prone, workflow, runtime, integration, UI/UX, automation, plugin, infra, Kubernetes, migration, or boundary-sensitive work; require bounded research.md, prior-art.md, and ux-research.md when interface behavior is affected.

Run the deterministic design artifact gate before Spec Kit plan, tasks, analyze, and implementation for workflow, orchestration, hook, roadmap, role gate, validator, runtime contract, operator/developer interaction, or UI/UX flow changes.

Run the deterministic prototype/spike gate before design, Spec Kit plan, tasks, analyze, and implementation when research or design leaves unresolved high-risk uncertainty that can be cheaply tested; keep prototype output separate from durable implementation.
Use $platform-role-governance before platform-part edits and route through exactly one primary specialist or helper role.

Keep plugin artifacts, assignment packets, subagent task text, and subagent messages in English. Use strict concise entity-bound terms such as local_cd and kubernetes_deployment. Do not use generic deploy.
Do not add sample, example, or illustrative sections. Repo-proof validation covers repo artifacts only and does not claim live runtime chat proof.

Validate overlay assets and language-policy coverage with `python3 scripts/validate_overlay.py --json validate --strict-overlay-skills`; add `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py validate`, `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py merge-authority-check --packet <path> --expected-repository <owner/repo> --expected-pr-number <number> --expected-head-ref <head-ref> --expected-head-sha <head-sha> --expected-base-ref <base-ref>`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py validate`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py inspect --repo <repo> --allowed-path <path> --json`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py closeout-preflight --repo <repo> --allowed-path <path> --expected-branch-prefix <prefix> --gitlink-proof <path>:<old-object>:<target-object>:<source-pr-merge-commit> --json`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py branch-inventory --repo <repo> --base <base> --json`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py branch-closeout-gate --repo <repo> --base <base> --github-prs-json <prs.json> --json`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py branch-prefix-check --branch <branch> --assignment-packet <assignment.json> --json`, `python3 scripts/git_discipline.py gitlink-audit --repo <parent-repo> --tree-ref <ref> --path <gitlink-path> --expected-target <sha> --local-checkout <path> --json`, and `python3 scripts/roadmap_control.py validate` when those governance surfaces change.
In subagent mode keep the parent agent orchestration-only: route, split, assign, wait or integrate evidence, run validators, close or report; no file writes, git mutations, or implementation tools.

Use the exact agent runtime matrix: main agent gpt-5.5 with medium; audit, complex-task, and subagent-spawning agents gpt-5.5 with high; file, log, and information/evidence gathering agents gpt-5.4-mini with medium; operator wording middle maps to Codex schema value medium.

Request plugin control checks through governed validation hook_id values only; allowed hooks map to repo-local Python scripts with fixed args, return bounded_json or concise_text evidence with hook_id, cwd, command_id, exit_code, sanitized_summary, and validation_target, and reject arbitrary shell, inline commands, raw output, env reads, credentials, raw logs, and production data.

Use `python3 scripts/agent_registration_sync.py check --target user --json` for read-only Codex custom-agent registration drift evidence; use `python3 scripts/agent_registration_sync.py audit-roles --json` for sequential role audit evidence; use `sync --target user` or `sync --target repo` only for explicit materialization into Codex agent directories, and do not claim plugin-root agent TOML files are auto-discovered. Validator also requires each canonical agent developer_instructions block to name its own agent and include the exact `- Role override: <description>` line.

In subagent mode use the parent control lane only for route and role selection, assignment packets, named validation hooks and validators, status checks, exit codes, bounded summaries, git status and changed-file names, operator-requested GitHub planning issues, evidence integration, and subagent closeout; do not use it for implementation authority, file writes, implementation commands, git add, git commit, git push, pull request mutation without explicit operator request, broad file-content collection, raw secrets, raw logs, raw chat, raw VPN configs, or production data.

Start roadmap work only through /goal. Before spawn/reuse/manage/close subagents, run the roadmap pre-task hook and capture missing-data and drift answers. Treat hard_max_active_subagents=100 as an absolute safety cap, use the lower active-executing worker cap for active work, and reuse only same-role same-repo workers with compatible write scope, no restricted-data taint, a compact continuation packet, and successful session runtime validation before session reuse or fork.

Use bounded no-subagent mode only for side answers, question-only explanations, one read-only status command, bounded repo inspection without mutation, or small exact-file bugfix admission; keep required role gates; skip non-product audit subagents for read-only or no-op turns; upgrade to normal gated mode before mutation; block no-subagent mode when the operator requests subagents.
Validate roadmap-control state with `assets/catalog/roadmap-control.v1.json` using `python3 scripts/roadmap_control.py validate`.
Use $yandex360-dns for bears.ru DNS governance through runtime-injected Infisical presence checks, dry-run plan review, and read-only evidence only.

Use $secret-factory only for catalog-listed local generation kinds; write to Infisical without reading, printing, storing, logging, committing, or exposing generated values, and return provider handoff for provider-owned values.
Use $project-mandate only after scripts/project_registry_gate.py allows the /srv/bears target path.

For non-product work, run the four audits at the stage boundary for plugin fit, drift, docs plus restricted-data safety, and user-information capture; accept legacy post-task wording only as an alias.

Route Sentry runtime-plugin capability work through $platform-role-governance; keep Sentry access code outside /srv/bears/plugins/bears, return redacted-only evidence by default, and require explicit operator approval plus security review for raw payloads, settings mutation, credential handling changes, production data exposure, or .226 Kubernetes lane promotion.

For Agent GitHub dev CD issue pickup, recognize only fixed GitHub issue identifiers `type:bugfix`, `type:idea`, and `type:develop-ready`; `type:develop-ready` is produced from repository constitution alignment, research, and accepted operator decisions.

agent pickup may start bounded development only for `type:develop-ready` after route gate, constitution evidence, research evidence, accepted decision evidence, owning role, task packet, duplicate guard, and `verify-agent-pickup --dry-run` pass; block unlabeled, idea-only, bugfix-only, blocked, human-review (`needs-human` or `manual-only`), secret, credentials, deploy, production, and security-review issues.

Before PR ready or merge in Agent GitHub dev CD, require `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py merge-authority-check --packet <path> --expected-repository <owner/repo> --expected-pr-number <number> --expected-head-ref <head-ref> --expected-head-sha <head-sha> --expected-base-ref <base-ref>` with exact PR/head, green checks or named no-CI exception/operator override, state_file_policy PASS, workflow_state and merge_authority_state refs, title validation, draft policy, rollback note, and operator_request or contract_authority. Plain parent send_input merge text is workflow drift.

Use $development-workflow-orchestration for broad Bears development work; parent agents must not implement broad work directly. Validate packets with `python3 scripts/development_workflow_validate.py validate`, validate the canonical map with `python3 scripts/agent_workflow_map.py validate`, and keep artifacts metadata-only.
Use the agentic enterprise workflow: one small repo-domain scope, research first, decision log, owner lineage, fast hooks, and `python3 scripts/agentic_enterprise_workflow.py validate` in CI.

Classify startup prompts with `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py classify-task --prompt-file <path>` before agent GitHub dev CD work; verify branch, merge, local_cd, and production-mutation boundaries with `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py verify-scenario-policy --packet <path>`.

Before automatic `dev` merge in Agent GitHub dev CD, require `python3 scripts/agent_github_dev_cd.py verify-dev-auto-merge --packet <path> --expected-repository <owner/repo> --expected-pr-number <number> --expected-head-ref <head-ref> --expected-head-sha <head-sha> --expected-base-ref dev` with contract_authority, state_file_policy PASS, workflow_state and merge_authority_state refs, local-commit-validation PASS, draft false, exact head, and CLEAN mergeable state.

Record local validation failure, workflow defect, deferred dirty, or runtime degradation in `assets/catalog/tech-debt-matrix.v1.json`; closure requires state-file refs and exact local-commit-validation evidence for affected merge scopes.

Use main-only plugin delivery: commit task changes to main, require local commit validation PASS for the exact SHA, run or rely on `python3 scripts/plugin_cache_sync.py watch --interval-seconds 15 --timeout-seconds 1200`, and close only when plugin-cache-sync state has delivery_complete=true with effective hooks proof; the Stop hook may block explicit closeout intent until that state is present.

Use @Bears Git/CD contracts for Kubernetes CD: Git policy maps dev-to-main and targets, the CD contract owns ordered deploy steps, and production apply runs automatically from GitHub Actions on main; do not add manual CD approval gates or local-agent deploy execution.
interface.longDescription: Bears workspace governance overlay for Spec Kit, roles, workflow, blockers, deploy gates, deterministic roadmap control, infrastructure network docs, Yandex 360 DNS skill governance, write-only Infisical creation governance, the project registry gate, bounded no-subagent mode for read-only no-op turns, deterministic agent registration sync for canonical agent TOML files, the governed validation hook runner for named repo-local control checks, the parent control lane for route and validation actions, the reusable worker pool policy for active, idle, reusable, state-file-gated, and stale workers, the non-product stage-boundary audit subagent policy, deterministic research gates, prototype/spike gates, design artifact gates, governed Sentry runtime-plugin capability design, and the English-only entity-bound repo-proof language policy for artifacts and subagent messages, and a deterministic plugin constitution gate at assets/catalog/plugin-constitution.v1.json with scripts/plugin_constitution.py validate. It also governs fixed GitHub issue identifiers `type:bugfix`, `type:idea`, and `type:develop-ready`, and it allows agent pickup only for `type:develop-ready` after route, constitution, research, accepted operator decision, owning role, task packet, duplicate guard, and `verify-agent-pickup --dry-run` evidence. It requires `merge-authority-check --packet <path> --expected-repository <owner/repo> --expected-pr-number <number> --expected-head-ref <head-ref> --expected-head-sha <head-sha> --expected-base-ref <base-ref>` before PR ready or merge and treats plain parent send_input merge text as workflow drift. Merge authority comes only from machine-readable state files: `workflow_state` and `merge_authority_state`. It classifies startup prompts with `classify-task --prompt-file <path>` into `dev`, `prod`, `bugfix`, `hot_bugfix`, `issue`, and `goal` development_scenario tasks, splits conflicting markers into separate subagent tasks, and verifies scenario branch, merge, local_cd, and production-mutation boundaries with `verify-scenario-policy --packet <path>`. It requires `verify-dev-auto-merge --packet <path> --expected-repository <owner/repo> --expected-pr-number <number> --expected-head-ref <head-ref> --expected-head-sha <head-sha> --expected-base-ref dev` before automatic `dev` merge; the packet must pass state_file_policy and local commit validation checks. It also governs the agentic enterprise workflow in assets/catalog/agentic-enterprise-workflow.v1.json and assets/catalog/agentic-enterprise-constitution.v1.json: four repo domains, small scopes, owner lineage, decision logs, clarification gate, fast hooks, Stop closeout delivery guard, and runtime degradation control. Local commit validation and operator-dispatched GitHub diagnostics validate the surface with python3 scripts/agentic_enterprise_workflow.py validate and python3 scripts/tech_debt_matrix.py validate. Main-only Bears plugin delivery is governed by assets/catalog/plugin-cache-sync.v1.json, scripts/local_commit_validation.py, and scripts/plugin_cache_sync.py: task commits target main, local Codex cache sync runs only after exact local commit validation success, and closeout requires runtime/plugin-cache-sync/plugin-cache-sync-state.v1.json with delivery_complete=true plus hooks.json and hooks/ proof. It owns machine-readable Git/CD deployment contracts in assets/catalog/git-deploy-contract.v1.json and assets/catalog/cd-kube-deploy-contract.v1.json, and scripts/bears_auto_cd.py is the fixed GitHub Actions executor for automatic Kubernetes CD from the infra repo main branch; CD contracts must not contain branch, merge-request, pull-request, or manual approval policy.
interface.shortDescription: Bears workspace governance overlay for Spec Kit, roles, workflow, blockers, deploy gates, roadmap control, no-subagent mode, agent registration sync, validation hook runner, parent control lane, reusable worker pool policy, research gates, Sentry runtime-plugin governance, and Secret Factory governance with plugin constitution checks. Adds Agent GitHub dev CD scenario classification, scenario-policy checks, issue-type governance, agent develop-ready pickup gates, state-file merge authority, dev auto-merge checks, and agentic enterprise 4-domain workflow guards and workflow technical-debt tracking Main-only plugin delivery requires exact local commit validation PASS, cache sync, and effective hooks proof. Adds machine-readable Git/CD contracts for automatic Kubernetes CD from main.

constitutionCheck.lifecyclePosition: after route gate and before research gate

name: bears
